intro: Padel tennis ranking is built on tournament points earned throughout the year. Each tournament offers different point values based on tier and finishing position. Discover exactly how many points you need to advance to the next APT/AAP category. Our calculator uses current 2026 point thresholds and is updated regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **points lookup table by category**. Enter your current rank to find exactly how many tournament points you need to advance in APT/AAP padel rankings.
useCases: Padel players planning tournament strategy to advance categories
Quick reference for APT/AAP advancement thresholds
explanation:
Padel tennis ranking combines competitive play, consistent tournament participation, and point accumulation. Points earned depend on tournament tier and your finishing position.

## How the Calculator Works

This calculator shows the exact points needed to advance from your current APT/AAP category based on 2026 ranking thresholds. Point values vary by tournament: winners earn ~200 points, finalists ~150 points, and semifinalists ~80 points.

## Important Notes

This calculator is an informational reference guide. For official ranking decisions and thresholds, consult the APT or AAP official websites. Point values and requirements are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What are the APT/AAP padel ranking categories?
faq.a: Categories range from Seventh (lowest) to First (highest), representing different skill and competitive levels. First category includes elite professional players.

faq.q: How many points do I earn for winning a tournament?
faq.a: Points depend on tournament tier. Winners typically earn around 200 points, finalists ~150 points, and semifinalists ~80 points.

faq.q: When is the official ranking updated?
faq.a: Official APT/AAP rankings are published monthly. Your ranking updates as tournament results are officially confirmed.

faq.q: How many tournaments do I need to play to advance?
faq.a: It depends on your current category and tournament tier. The calculator estimates based on typical point values—you may need 8-10 tournaments for mid-level advancement.
A Masters is the season-ending championship event featuring the top-ranked players of the year across all categories.

faq.q: Can I drop to a lower ranking category?
faq.a: Yes, if you play fewer tournaments or earn poor results, you can drop to a lower category in the next official ranking update.

faq.q: What's the difference between AAP and APT?
faq.a: AAP (Argentine Padel Association) governs padel in Argentina. APT is the international professional padel circuit with global tournaments.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no account or registration.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
